info.title: Taxes
info.x-hubspot-api-use-case: You want to include a percentage sales tax on quotes, depending on state requirements.
info.x-hubspot-introduction: Use the taxes API to create and manage taxes that can be included in quotes.

post.summary: Read a batch of taxes by ID

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Returns a batch of tax objects matching the provided internal IDs or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read an Object identified by `{taxId}`. `{taxId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param.  Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: taxId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tax record to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object type

delete.description: Move an Object identified by `{taxId}` to the recycling bin.

parameters.name: taxId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tax record to archive.

patch.description: Perform a partial update of an Object identified by `{taxId}`. `{taxId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. Provided property values will be overwritten. Read-only and non-existent properties will be ignored. Properties values can be cleared by passing an empty string.

parameters.name: taxId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tax record to update.

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object type

post.summary: Archive a batch of taxes by ID
post.description: Archives the specified batch of tax records by ID. Returns no content on success.
Returns the newly created batch of tax objects, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.
Returns the updated batch of tax objects, including any partial success details for multi-status responses.

get.description: Read a page of taxes. Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource will be returned as the `paging.next.after` JSON property of a paged response containing more results

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored. Usage of this parameter will reduce the maximum number of objects that can be read by a single request

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Create a tax with the given properties and return a copy of the object, including the ID. Documentation and examples for creating standard taxes is provided.
Create or update records identified by a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object.
Returns a collection of tax objects matching the specified search criteria and filters.
